app.name: OxideTerm
app.one_liner: All-in-one terminal workspace — local shells, SSH, SFTP, remote IDE, AI agent, and file manager in a single native binary.

screens.name: Main Terminal Workspace
screens.purpose: Hosts active terminal sessions, file browser, and AI agent interactions in a tabbed layout.

screens.name: New Connection Modal
screens.purpose: Allows user to configure and initiate SSH or local shell sessions.

rust_commands.name: create_session
rust_commands.purpose: Initiates a new terminal session (local or SSH) based on user-provided configuration.

rust_commands.name: save_connection_profile
rust_commands.purpose: Persists connection settings for future reuse.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build new connection modal UI
week 1: Implement core terminal rendering with xterm-js
week 1: Integrate russh for SSH connectivity
week 2: Enable AI agent integration stub
acceptance_tests: After a failed connection attempt, error is clearly displayed and user can retry without app crash.
Application runs as a single native binary on macOS, Windows, and Linux without requiring OpenSSL or Electron.
Saved connection profiles persist across application restarts.
User can manually input SSH connection details in the address bar or modal and successfully establish a session.
